ADR-007 written in docs/adr/0007-schema-evolution-and-type-governance.md. Covers: schema versioning via MIGRATIONS, type registry via SchemaConfig, experimental→registered→canonical migration path, graduated validation policy (advisory/lenient/strict), domain separation principle (domains extend never override). References ADR-001, ADR-005, ADR-006. Also added ADR-006 and ADR-007 summaries to docs/adr/README.md.
All 7 children complete: confidence_chain, contradictions, composite_score, detect_trend, ADR-007, SSE /events filter, CONTRADICTS edge type. Epic fully delivered.

Temporal confidence decay for edges (query_stale_edges) and observations (query_observations with days_since_last_observation decay) is implemented. query_stale_edges: computes effective_confidence = confidence * 0.5^(age_days/half_life) with per-layer half-lives (L1/L2=inf, L3=90d, L4=30d). apply_confidence_decay updates stale edges. CLI: ohm graph decay --threshold. SDK: get_stale_edges() and apply_confidence_decay(). PERT columns (0.15.0 migration) support full VoI distribution. decay_lambda field per node is not needed for edge decay; layer-based half-lives are sufficient. Observation decay (e^(-λ×days)) for observations aging is optionally design-extendable.

Implemented source reliability weighting for compound_confidence. When source_weights is provided (dict mapping source_agent -> p_accurate), observations from reliable sources count more: weighted_geometric_mean = 1 - Π(1-p_i)^w_i. Unknown sources get default weight=0.5. An observation from a reliable source (0.9) counts 1.8x more than unknown (0.5). Works with independent, correlated, and partial correlation modes. Backward compatible (weighted=False when source_weights=None). Tests: test_compound_confidence_source_weighting, test_compound_confidence_unknown_source_default_weight, test_compound_confidence_weighted_correlated, test_compound_confidence_no_weights_backward_compat.

Implemented PERT variance-based VoI uncertainty in compute_voi(). Wide PERT bounds (p05=0.1, p95=0.9) now correctly rank higher than tight bounds. Fallback to 1-confidence when PERT data unavailable. 3 new tests added (test_voi_pert_uncertainty_wide_bounds_rank_higher, test_voi_uncertainty_fallback_without_pert, test_voi_uncertainty_zero_when_pert_tight). All 18 VoI tests pass. Commit: 3ff7ab8

Fixed weighted compound_confidence formula from (1-c)^w to linear scaling 1-Π(1-w*c). The old formula had no probabilistic interpretation. New formula correctly combines weighted probabilities: P=1-Π(1-w_i*c_i). Tests: 12 compound_confidence tests pass including bug fix verification and weight clamping.

Fixed: (1) SDK connect_http() now forces UTF-8 decoding with Latin-1 fallback and warning log, (2) server.py _json_response() now sends charset=utf-8, (3) Added 3 Unicode round-trip tests (café, emoji, mixed). All 140 SDK tests pass.

Fixed: BFS hops increased 3→6 for longer causal chains; added connectivity diagnostic in compute_ate — when ATE=0 and cause not an ancestor of effect, returns error with explanation instead of misleading ATE=0.0/RR=1.0
Won't fix: 404 is correct behavior when POST /edge uses a non-existent node ID. The node 'concept-demand-rationing' doesn't exist — the actual node has a different ID. This is expected validation.

By design: health_score = 1 - (orphans+low_conf+stale)/total_nodes. The formula intentionally doesn't make orphan rate the sole determining factor — a graph with many high-confidence connected nodes can still score 0.73 with 25% orphans.

Data quality issue, not a code bug: suggest_causes uses graph topology which doesn't know about semantic domains. Mixed domains (college/career mixed with geopolitics) are expected when data from multiple domains co-exists. Future enhancement: domain filtering.
Umbrella issue: all addressable sub-items from Metis's second 32-endpoint test run are now fixed. 10 bugs fixed in batch (OHM-7308..7321), 2 closed as by-design, 1 as data quality, 1 as won't-fix.

Fixed: compute_voi now always includes sensitivity_methods_used and mixed_sensitivity_methods in response. When rankings mix ATE and path_confidence, mixed_sensitivity_methods=True with an explanatory note. All early-return paths also include these fields.

Fixed: compute_voi accepts min_observations param (default 0 = no flagging). Nodes below threshold get low_data_warning=True and low_data_note in their ranking entry. Server wires ?min_observations=N from query string.
Fixed: query_agent_health node_agents CTE now strips 'agent-' prefix when checking for duplicates in ohm_agent_state, preventing both 'metis' and 'agent-metis' from appearing.

Fixed: _get_infra_health now calls query_graph_health and includes health_score, node_count, edge_count, orphan_count, orphan_rate, low_confidence_count under a 'graph' key. Wrapped in try/except so health check never fails if DB is slow.
False alarm — 'ate' key is consistent throughout. compute_ate returns {"ate": float} and all callers (compute_sensitivity, compute_voi) look up ate_result["ate"]. No inconsistency exists.

Audited all module-global state in src/ohm/. Webhook registry restructured to {customer_id: {agent: config}} — cross-tenant firing now impossible. All 8 _trigger_webhooks callers updated to pass customer_id=self._customer_id. SSE subscribers store customer_id for audit. Rate limit remains IP-keyed (DDoS protection; per-customer quota deferred to OHM-982m). Metrics remain aggregate (per-tenant in OHM-r0et). 3 new isolation tests verify tenant A webhook never fires on tenant B events. All 182 existing server tests pass.

Implemented: webhook registry restructured to {customer_id: {agent: config}}, all trigger callers updated, SSE subscribers store customer_id. 3 isolation tests pass. Rate limit IP-keyed by design (per-customer → OHM-982m). Metrics aggregate (per-tenant → OHM-r0et).

Strategy decided: per-tenant write mutex (threading.Lock, not RLock) in TenantManager, owned by TenantManager not OhmStore. Reads concurrent (DuckDB allows this). Writes serialize per-tenant, not globally. Documented in ADR-015. Implementation inside tss4.2 (TenantManager module). Existing OhmStore._lock (RLock) covers only execute()/execute_one(); the 93 direct conn.execute() calls will be absorbed into TenantManager's write-path lock.

Out of scope for application layer. HIPAA deployments must use filesystem-level encryption (LUKS, dm-crypt, cloud KMS volumes). OHM documents this requirement in the ops guide. Application-level field encryption rejected: partial coverage creates false compliance confidence. DuckDB EE deferred until a paying HIPAA customer with budget justifies the license. tss4.13 healthcare template is schema-only — no actual patient data stored by OHM.

Superseded: no external queue needed. Use ohm_messages DuckDB table in core store (id, customer_id, channel, payload_json, status, created_at, sent_at, error, retry_count). Background thread polls every 60s. Keeps architecture coherent — everything in DuckDB, no new infrastructure dependency. Message volume for SMB daily briefs does not justify a dedicated queue.

handlers/markov.py created with MarkovHandlerMixin; OhmHandler inherits it; methods removed from server.py. Pushed 65abced.

Fixed: record_request() now buffers counts in memory (_request_counts dict) instead of writing meta.json on every request. The checkpoint loop flushes buffered counts to disk every _checkpoint_interval seconds. check_quota('requests_per_day') merges in-memory counts with on-disk counts. Eliminates hot-write bottleneck at enterprise tier (OHM-8d54).

Fixed: replaced 100ms sleep heuristic with _wait_for_file_ready() using exponential-backoff retry (0.05s base, 10 attempts max). Cross-platform: only retries on Windows file-locked errors (IOError/OSError).
Fixed: same as OHM-bieb. Exponential backoff ensures file handles are released before copy.
Fixed: backup_tenant now reads file into memory, writes to .tmp, then os.replace(). If crash occurs during write, the original file is untouched. No more corrupt partial backups.
Fixed: backup_tenant now re-inserts the store into the cache after backup completes. Prevents cold-start + re-open race.
Fixed: added SHA-256 checksums for db/wal/meta files in backup metadata. Each _atomic_copy() returns (size, sha256). Checksums stored in backup meta.json.
Fixed: same as OHM-g7zc. Store re-inserted after backup with proper locking.

Not a code bug. Manufacturing template has 26 node types appropriate for SMB scope (process, sensor, machine, workstation, etc.). The issue raised a question about whether manufacturing should inherit all 18 topo-specific types (controller, valve, pump, etc.), but this is a design decision, not a defect. The manufacturing template intentionally includes the types needed for discrete manufacturing workflows. If full topo inheritance is desired, a follow-up feature issue should be created.

Fixed: _delete_node and _delete_edge in server.py now call check_can_delete_node() and check_can_delete_edge() from the boundary module. The inline ownership checks have been replaced with boundary module calls, consistent with ADR-003. (OHM-dtoc)

Added seed_agents to SchemaConfig and _seed_domain_agents() function. When a tenant is provisioned with a domain template (home_services, healthcare, manufacturing, construction, ohm), the specified agent role nodes are pre-seeded in both ohm_nodes and ohm_agent_state tables. Templates updated with appropriate roles: dispatch_analyst/compliance_planner/operations_manager/parts_broker/schedule_coordinator for home_services; billing_specialist/clinical_coordinator/practice_manager for healthcare; process_engineer/maintenance_supervisor/shift_supervisor for manufacturing; project_manager/safety_officer/estimator for construction; metis/clio/socrates/hephaestus/deepthought for OHM.

Created ADR-016 documenting Quack production readiness criteria: (1) extension stability requires DuckDB stable release with Quack (currently only core_nightly), (2) security requires 32+ char tokens via env vars and TLS proxy, (3) test coverage requires passing concurrent write test (currently failing — blocks activation), (4) ohmd config flag quack:true to opt-in, (5) 4-phase activation path from mutex to Quack. Also added Quack notes to CLAUDE.md.

Implemented: added scale field to observations (probability/count/currency/percent/unknown), validated value range for probability scale, filtered non-probability observations from Bayesian priors, added migration 0.19.0

fix: use PRAGMA table_info for dynamic DuckLake column discovery — auto-restore now uses column intersection instead of hardcoded columns

All 3 VE performance fixes complete. pgmpy pre-warm (5.3s→<1ms), parent capping (131K→256 CPT entries), VE instance reuse in BayesianContext. 143 tests passing. First inference call now 80ms, cached <20ms.

Fixed in commit fd618c4. N-player games now properly handled. Extract_game validates 2-player constraint and compute_nash uses iterated dominance elimination for N>2. All game tests passing including test_extract_game_three_players.
Fixed in commit dc8899c. Tenant test now properly creates isolated tenant and verifies 5 seed agents, not 0 rows.
Completed: All 4 decision nodes have utility_usd_per_day values populated. VoI returns USD-normalized values.
Completed: Game theory N-player fix committed (fd618c4). All tests passing.

Completed: Granger causality, edge stability, and regime detection all implemented and accessible via API. Edge stability returns 0 scores with current data volume (min_windows=3 requirement), will populate as observations accumulate. /granger, /edge_stability, /regime endpoints all functional.

Implemented soft evidence from SUPPORTS/APPLIES_TO edges. API exposes ?soft_evidence=1&soft_edges=SUPPORTS,APPLIES_TO on /inference, /intervene, /voi endpoints. Tested: hormuz_and_gate P(good) shifts from 0.011 (without) to 0.133 (with soft evidence) — 12pp shift from previously invisible edges.
All 5 child issues resolved: OD01.1 (temporal analysis), OD01.2 (game theory), OD01.6 (utility data gap) closed as done. OD01.4 (structure learning) and OD01.5 (POMDP) deprioritized but unblocked.

Fixed: DuckLake extension now loads via INSTALL ducklake FROM core; LOAD ducklake; in attach_ducklake() before the loaded check. Daemon successfully attaches DuckLake on startup. All three restore targets (nodes, edges, observations) now handled with column intersection for schema compatibility.

Implemented: /neighborhood returns citation_status on L3 edges. verified if L2 REFERENCES edge exists, unverified otherwise.

Admin endpoints for backfill created.
Completed: 75 source nodes created for Hormuz and all other observations. 100% source URL coverage. 365 REFERENCES edges (L2) connecting concepts to sources. L3:L2 ratio improved from 31:1 to 2.1:1.

ADR-013 Source Citation Architecture fully implemented: (1) Schema enforces source_url on source node creation, (2) API returns citation_status on L3 edges, (3) Source trigger added to AGENTS.md, (4) Socrates challenge protocol updated, (5) ADR-015 written, (6) Observation sources migrated to node references, (7) 75 source nodes backfilled with URLs, (8) 365 REFERENCES edges in L2. PERT infrastructure complete: auto-backfill endpoint, 453 edges populated (161 from observations, 292 from confidence), neighborhood query returns PERT fields, VoI/inference use distributions. Agent prompts are the remaining work, not infrastructure.
Fix N+1 queries: batch label/urgency lookups with IN clause + dict lookup instead of per-row SELECT.
Replace exponential path-enumerating BFS with O(V+E) memoized DP approach. Constant max_depth=10 instead of len(adj)+2. Tracks best max-variance path per node.
Replace unbounded dict with _LRUBayesianCache (maxsize=50). Evicts oldest entry when exceeded. Preserves dict interface for existing callers (clear, in, len, []).

Replace Python-loop decay computation with single SQL CTE using power(), date_diff(), and CASE WHEN. Configurable half_life_days via dynamic CASE clauses. Filters soft-deleted edges. Adds regression test for custom half-life overrides.
Batch node lookups via single get_nodes(ids=...) call before loop; move get_edges for concept labels outside loop and index by from_node. Eliminates N SQL roundtrips per ranking entry.
Replace N per-candidate get_observations() calls with single SQL GROUP BY node_id query using IN clause. Zero-observation nodes default via setdefault().

Replace N per-node infer.query() calls with single batch infer.query(variables=all_nodes) + marginalize() to extract per-node marginals. Same fix applied to both causal_intervention() and BayesianContext.intervention(), covering both the do-operator and observation comparison paths. Fallback to individual queries if batch fails.

Add structured error handling to /granger, /edge_stability, and /suggest endpoints. Validation errors return 400, internal errors return 500 with structured JSON instead of unhandled exception propagation. Added regression tests for invalid parameters and node IDs.
Already fixed: test_granger_endpoint passes (4 granger tests, all pass). Likely resolved by a prior change.

All 5 acceptance criteria met:
1. /discover?nodes=X,Y,Z&method=pc returns candidate edges with confidence
2. Discovery works on binary observation data (0/1 threshold)
3. Candidate edges queued via /discover?queue=true; reviewed via GET /discover/queue and POST /discover/queue/review (accept→CAUSES/CORRELATES_WITH, reject)
4. Insufficient data returns graceful error with metadata
5. PC→GES fallback: when PC produces 0 edges, auto-falls back to GES

Additional fixes:
- Auto-select N+1 fixed with batch get_observation_counts() on GraphReader protocol
- Discovery queue table (schema v0.20.0)
- Structured error handling on /discover (400/500)
- 15 new tests (all passing)

Implemented in commit 11df946. _semantic_auto_link_fragment uses embedding cosine similarity (>0.7 threshold), falls back to _substring_auto_link_fragment when Ollama unavailable. provenance='auto_link_semantic' vs 'auto_link_substring'. Tests pass.

Semantic auto-linking makes min label length less critical — semantic matching doesn't depend on substring length. Lowering threshold is now low-priority since embeddings handle short labels. Keeping issue open but deprioritized.

Implemented in commit 11df946. /stats?include_l0=true now returns fragment_density dict with fragments_total, fragments_with_links, total_fragment_edges, and density ratio. Live verified: fragment_density=1.2.

Implemented: batch_orphan_triage (queries, SDK, HTTP handler), orphan_type_breakdown in health metrics, orphan_rate_warning in heartbeat when >10%. 15 tests passing. Agents can now call GET /admin/orphan-triage or graph.orphan_triage() to get link suggestions for orphan nodes.

Implemented in 2d1a9ab. Core write-through wrapper, metadata propagation, retrieval, and HTTP endpoints are in place with tests. Remaining work (real AWS e2e, IAM docs, retrieval-to-graph edges) can be tracked as follow-up tasks.

Fixed in 305230a. Post-write suggestions now run synchronously under the request thread's write lock with a deadline budget, eliminating the cross-thread DuckDB access that caused segfaults. Reopen if segfaults recur in other background threads or under production load.

Default pytest suite is green and substantially optimized: 2567 passed, 6 skipped, 109 deselected, 32 warnings in ~16:49 (down from ~26:00). Key changes: skip slow/benchmark tests by default, disable suggestions by default, fix concurrent server threading, move pgmpy prewarm to conftest import time, and add OHM_DISABLE_EMBEDDINGS guard. Remaining slow tests are CLI subprocess integration and tenant LRU internals, which are optimization opportunities, not blockers.

Verified docs/testing/ohm-feature-coverage.md covers all required deliverables: matrix across ADR-028..037 + 3 in-flight features (unit/HTTP/adversarial/perf/agent-campaign/gaps columns), top-5 untested behaviors, prioritized next-investment table. Report is first-pass; explicit regeneration trigger noted when OHM-9zae/OHM-bsse land.

Sync drift cleanup: OHM-k0bi was closed in JSONL (committed 589b214) but the local Dolt DB still showed it open/in_progress. Code fix landed in 305230a (synchronous post-write suggestions under request write lock with 0.5s deadline budget, ThreadPoolExecutor removed). Full default pytest suite verified passing (2567 passed, 6 skipped, 109 deselected) at close time. Re-import of JSONL did not reconcile status, so closing manually to restore canonical state from the git-tracked export.

Hardened /orient endpoint against 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable 500s. Defensive fix in src/ohm/server/handlers/analysis.py:1061 — _get_orient now wraps fetchone() in a _scalar helper (returns None when fetchone() returns None), guards the sparse_nodes r[4]<=1 filter against NULL edge_count, defends island iteration against dict/None, and uses a _coerce_iso helper for datetime/None/str coercion. Added 4 regression tests in tests/test_server.py::TestOrientEndpoint covering: unknown agent (cold_start), missing agent param (400), seeded activity (full packet), empty DB (no fetchone crash). All 4 new tests pass; 91 of 91 non-hook server tests pass (6 hook test failures pre-exist on main).

Implemented outcome feedback loop MVP. Schema 0.36.0 adds expected_claim, success_criteria, outcome, outcome_notes columns to ohm_nodes. New query_close_task_with_outcome in queries/__init__.py atomically sets task_status=done, records outcome on the task node, and (when expected_claim or claim_node is set + outcome is TRUE/FALSE) records an ohm_outcomes row via query_record_outcome with source_agent=task.created_by. New HTTP endpoint POST /tasks/{id}/outcome. PATCH /node and PATCH /tasks/{id} now accept expected_claim and success_criteria. SDK (both direct Graph and HTTP client) gains complete_task_with_outcome(). 20/20 new tests in tests/test_task_outcome.py pass; 376/376 schema+queries+server tests pass. Deferred to follow-up: VERIFIED_BY/FALSIFIED_BY edge types, automatic Bayesian reliability cascade, write_node signature extension for expected_claim.

Implemented Beads → OHM task sync MVP. New module src/ohm/integrations/beads_sync.py with sync_beads_to_ohm_tasks() — mirrors assigned Beads issues into OHM task nodes. Field mapping: bd.id→metadata.beads_id, title→label, description→content, status→task_status (via BD_STATUS_MAP), priority 0-4→P0-P3, assignee→assigned_to (strips @olympus.local), labels→tags. Creates a beads-backlog-anchor concept node for cross-link enforcement (ADR-018). Status precedence prevents OHM-side status regressions (done stays done even if Beads reopens). Idempotent: re-sync updates existing tasks. New HTTP endpoint POST /admin/sync-beads accepts explicit issues list or fetches via bd CLI / JSONL fallback. 20/20 tests pass in tests/test_beads_sync.py (3 assignee normalization, 13 direct query, 4 HTTP integration). 285/285 regression tests pass. Deferred to follow-up: OHM→Beads bidirectional sync, webhook-triggered real-time sync, closed/duplicate cleanup, CLI command wrapper.

Implemented in commit 44c215b. GET /documents/{id} returns JSON metadata + enriches with source_node; GET /documents/{id}/download streams raw bytes with Content-Disposition. Route, handler, store methods (get/get_record/exists on Local/S3/Bedrock stores), and 3 dedicated tests (test_get_document_metadata, test_get_document_download, test_get_missing_document_returns_404) all in place and passing. Related packaging gap (missing markdown dep for .md ingestion) filed as OHM-qmmw — out of scope for this ticket.
Fixed inline with OHM-qmmw — bumping the two assertions to 0.36.0.

Fixed: (1) added markdown>=3.6 to the ingestion optional-dependency group in pyproject.toml; (2) made parse_markdown_to_tree in src/ohm/ingestion/document_tree.py degrade gracefully when the markdown package is absent — caches a module-level _markdown_lib flag, falls back to a single-paragraph DocumentTree (with first-line '# heading' title extraction via regex) so text extraction still works and uploads no longer 500. Added 2 fallback tests. Also fixed pre-existing schema-version test drift in tests/test_decision_nodes.py and tests/test_hypothesis_tree.py (SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 0.36.0 in 8a3fe52 but assertions were never updated). 16 doc/parser tests + 20 schema-primitive tests all green.

Implemented in commit 72c483a. GET /changes consolidates 6 poll-style endpoints (/listen, /contradictions, /anomalies, /stale, /suggest, /tasks) into a single personalized agent delta. New query_agent_changes() in src/ohm/graph/queries/__init__.py returns legacy core fields always (since, agent, query_timestamp, node_total, edge_total, nodes, edges) and adds 5 agent-scoped sections when an agent is resolvable: new_observations_on_my_nodes, edges_touching_my_nodes, challenges_to_my_edges, tasks_assigned_or_status_changed, stale_nodes_needing_refresh. Handler refactor preserves backward compatibility: since is now optional (falls back to last_sync then 24h ago); agent falls back to authenticated principal; validate_timestamp applied. SDK Graph.changes() and RemoteGraph.changes() added. 13 new tests (9 HTTP integration + 4 SDK), no regressions. Pre-existing data_products failures unchanged (missing jsonschema locally).

S3/MinIO backend was already implemented in commit d4eb203 (S3DocumentStore in src/ohm/documents/store.py:158 — boto3-based with bucket/prefix/endpoint config from env vars, supporting save/get/exists/get_record/update_metadata/delete). Ticket was never closed. Added 9 moto-backed roundtrip tests covering all store operations under tests/test_document_library.py::TestS3DocumentStore, plus moto>=5.0 and markdown>=3.6 to the dev extra in pyproject.toml so the integration suite is runnable end-to-end. Commit 108d558 pushed.

Completed in commit 43428c9. The weibull_shape column and confidence_at() Weibull path were already landed in v0.26.0 (OHM-24g9). This commit completes the remaining acceptance criteria: (1) create_observation() in queries/ now accepts and persists weibull_shape, auto-resolving from DEFAULT_WEIBULL_SHAPE; (2) get_observation_chain/get_active_observations pass weibull_shape to decay_profile; (3) implemented the ghost GET /observation/{id} route and added GET /observation/{id}/confidence?at=... returning effective_confidence, weibull_shape, half_life_days, decay_function, decay_profile, age_days, evaluated_at; (4) /admin/learned-half-lives now includes weibull_shape per obs_type via empirical_half_life(); (5) Graph.confidence_at() SDK method added. 25 new tests (17 Weibull formula + equivalence, 6 HTTP endpoint, 2 SDK), 2060 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit 661054c (src/ohm/documents/store.py:44 LocalDocumentStore, src/ohm/documents/extract.py PDF extraction, src/ohm/server/handlers/documents.py POST /documents/upload). All store/extract/ingest tests passing (verified across OHM-5srl, OHM-qmmw, and OHM-2ibj sessions).

PDF text extraction pipeline implemented in commit 661054c (src/ohm/documents/extract.py — pypdf + pdfplumber fallback, HTML/Markdown parsing via document_tree, text/plain passthrough). Tests passing in test_document_library.py::TestExtractText.

POST /documents/upload endpoint implemented in commit 661054c (src/ohm/server/handlers/documents.py — multipart upload + URL fetch, src/ohm/documents/store.py download handler). GET /documents/{id}/download also added in commit 44c215b (OHM-5srl).

Document-tree ingestion bridge implemented in src/ohm/ingestion/document_library_bridge.py (stage_documents() function — queue-based, handles url/local_path/content_bytes, POSTs to /documents/upload, moves items to source_created queue). Depends OHM-w76g and OHM-5bjt (both now closed) are satisfied.

Completed in commit 92e5e5e. The ohm_aliases + ohm_content_hashes tables, normalize_alias(), compute_content_hash(), register/resolve/query functions, detect_alias_duplicates(), and HTTP endpoints (GET /resolve, GET /admin/alias-duplicates, POST /admin/merge, POST /admin/backfill-aliases, POST /admin/backfill-content-hashes) were already implemented in v0.23.0 (OHM-g0kv). This commit fills the remaining gaps: (1) create_node() in queries/ now auto-registers aliases; (2) find_or_create_node() uses alias resolution before label matching; (3) new merge_nodes() in queries/ — accessible to SDK/CLI without HTTP; (4) new detect_semantic_duplicates() using embedding cosine similarity >= threshold; (5) new GET /admin/duplicates endpoint combining all 3 strategies; (6) SDK Graph.resolve_node(), Graph.merge_nodes(), Graph.find_duplicates(). 18 new tests, 2075 total passing, 0 regressions.

Completed in commit 0e99b6b. Extended VALID_HOOK_EVENTS with 7 ingestion-stage events (pre_fetch, post_fetch, pre_parse, post_parse, pre_commit, post_commit, on_error). Built IngestionPipeline in src/ohm/ingestion/pipeline.py — run_pipeline() orchestrates fetch→parse→commit with pre/post hooks at each stage. Pre-stage hooks that exit non-zero abort the pipeline (aborted_by_hook set). on_error fires on exceptions. CI mode (BEADS_HOOKS=0 or OHM_NO_HOOKS=1) bypasses all hooks. Added POST /admin/hooks/<event> HTTP endpoint for server-side hook execution. Added CLI: ohm hooks list, ohm hooks run. 25 new tests covering CI mode, event validation, pipeline basic flow, hook execution/abort/on_error, skip_hooks, and HookRecord validation. 2098 total passing, 0 regressions. Success criteria met: pre_fetch hook can compute content_hash and skip, post_commit hook can validate, failed hook aborts stage, CI mode bypasses, audit log persisted.

Implemented in commit b098091. query_neighborhood_narrative() in queries/__init__.py walks 1-hop edges, builds reasoning chains with path/edge/summary, gathers observations, generates connections_summary. GET /narrative/{node_id}?agent=NAME HTTP endpoint. Graph.narrative() SDK method. 16 new tests (11 unit + 3 HTTP + 2 SDK), 2113 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit bb155b6. query_claim_lineage() in queries/__init__.py — recursive CTE over provenance edges (DERIVES_FROM, REFERENCES, SUPPORTS, SUPPORTS_EVIDENCE, CONTRADICTS_EVIDENCE, TESTS), enriched with observations per node. Returns tree with confidence_chain products, source leaves, gap detection (nodes with no observations). GET /lineage/{node_id} HTTP endpoint. Graph.lineage() SDK method. 14 new tests (10 unit + 2 HTTP + 2 SDK), 2124 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit 220fc92. query_contradiction_summary() in queries/__init__.py — splits observations into above/below baseline groups, computes effective_confidence per observation using Weibull decay, builds 'sides' with agents and observation counts, finds CHALLENGED_BY edges, and generates a recommendation (stronger side / unresolved / review required). GET /contradiction/{node_id} HTTP endpoint. Graph.contradiction_summary() SDK method. 14 new tests (9 unit + 3 HTTP + 2 SDK), 2136 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit 1a7f939. query_task_context() in queries/__init__.py — fetches task node with all task fields, builds 2-hop subgraph via query_neighborhood(), traces rationale through DECISION_DEPENDS_ON/DERIVES_FROM/REFERENCES/SUPPORTS/TESTS edges, finds blocking tasks via DEPENDS_ON/BLOCKED_BY/BLOCKS edges, derives expected_outcome from success_criteria (with fallback to expected_claim). GET /task-context/{task_id} HTTP endpoint. Graph.task_context() SDK method. 14 new tests (10 unit + 2 HTTP + 2 SDK), 2148 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit f0775e5. query_confidence_report() in queries/__init__.py — finds shifted_beliefs (edges with updated_at > since, cross-referenced with challenges and outcomes for reason), new_beliefs (edges created since), and stale_beliefs (confidence < 0.15). Falls back to last_sync then 30d ago. GET /confidence-report?agent=NAME&since=ISO8601 HTTP endpoint. Graph.confidence_report() SDK method. 11 new tests (7 unit + 2 HTTP + 2 SDK), 2157 total passing, 0 regressions.

Epic complete — all 5 child issues closed. (1) Neighborhood narrative: GET /narrative/{id} — reasoning chains, evidence, connections summary. (2) Claim lineage: GET /lineage/{id} — provenance tree with confidence products, gap detection, source leaves. (3) Contradiction summary: GET /contradiction/{id} — both-sides view with effective confidence (Weibull decay), recommendation. (4) Task context: GET /task-context/{id} — task + 2-hop subgraph + rationale + blocking. (5) Confidence report: GET /confidence-report?agent=NAME — shifted/new/stale beliefs with reasons. All 5 surfaces have queries/ functions, HTTP endpoints, and SDK methods. 69 new tests across 5 commits (b098091, bb155b6, 220fc92, 1a7f939, f0775e5). 2157 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit 5a353e8 (rebased to e709b65). query_counterfactual_cascade() + query_compare_scenarios() in queries/__init__.py — BFS cascade with edge_overrides, node_interventions (do-operator), disabled_edges, disabled_nodes. POST /scenario HTTP endpoint. Graph.scenario() SDK method. 18 new tests (13 unit + 3 HTTP + 2 SDK), 2172 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit 2cb7346 (rebased to abc4f8f). Added 3 new node types (scenario, action, intervention) to VALID_NODE_TYPES and MUST_HAVE_EDGE_NODE_TYPES. Added 7 new edge types: L3 (COUNTERFACTUAL_OF, PROPOSES_ACTION, EVALUATES) and L4 (PROPOSED_BY, EXECUTED_BY, FEEDBACK_TO, INTERVENES_ON). Schema bumped to 0.37.0 with migration entry. JSON templates updated. 28 new tests + 4 schema-version updates, 2199 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit 546a8a7. Added gate_type and gate_status columns to ohm_nodes, constraint_expr column to ohm_edges. VALID_GATE_TYPES ({AND, OR}) and VALID_GATE_STATUSES ({intact, converted, compromised, failed}) validation constants. Schema bumped to 0.38.0 with migration (columns + partial indexes). 12 new tests in TestGateGovernance + 3 schema-version updates, 2211 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented in commit b2eafa7 (rebased to 865be77). propose_action() creates action node + PROPOSES_ACTION edge. execute_action() sets status to executed, records outcome, creates EXECUTED_BY edge. query_loop_status() returns proposed/executed actions with outcome counts and recent scenarios. POST /propose-action, POST /execute-action, GET /loop-status HTTP endpoints. Graph.propose_action(), Graph.execute_action(), Graph.loop_status() SDK methods. 19 new tests (10 unit + 3 HTTP + 3 SDK + 3 existing), 2223 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented as part of OHM-446a (commit b2eafa7). GET /loop-status endpoint and Graph.loop_status() SDK method provide the agent-readable loop status — proposed/executed actions, recent scenarios, and outcome summary. No separate implementation needed — the loop status endpoint was built as part of the action API.

The Industrial Agent Manifesto conformance checklist depends on the autonomy loop API (OHM-446a), now closed. The schema primitives (gate_type, gate_status, constraint_expr, action/intervention/scenario node types) and the propose/execute/loop-status API are all in place. The conformance checklist can be written as a separate doc/test suite against these primitives.

Completed in commit 44d49ff (rebased to 83e3960). Reconciled Metis design notes with upstream code: (1) Added 'twin' node type to VALID_NODE_TYPES and MUST_HAVE_EDGE_NODE_TYPES — twins are first-class nodes linked via EVALUATES edge. (2) Reconciled gate_status values — added Metis aliases (open/closed/stuck) alongside existing (intact/converted/compromised/failed). (3) Updated all JSON templates. (4) Created industrial process acceptance test (OHM-brps) with 8 tests exercising AND-gate governance, constraint_expr, twin registration, counterfactual cascade, and full autonomy loop. (5) Documented reconciliation decisions: twin/predict feeds /scenario as edge_overrides, twin/constraints maps to constraint_expr, twin gate health maps to gate_type/gate_status. 2231 total passing, 0 regressions.

Implemented as part of OHM-8dg4 (commit 44d49ff). tests/test_industrial_process.py — 8 acceptance tests for chemical reactor with process state + inventory + market: AND-gate governance, constraint_expr, twin registration, baseline cascade, counterfactual feed disruption, full autonomy loop, compromised gate, gate_status aliases.
